missing_item_templates: EVG&D source basis from audited financial/P&L statement or internally audited management accounts
applicability of EVG&D source basis
guardrail_effects: EVG&D data should not be marked disclosed when source basis is absent; applicability check only applies to if-applicable language
rules.review_note: Remove requires_gender_breakdown and its missing-item/guardrail. Keep requires_source_basis; if desired, retain requires_applicability_check only for the phrase 'if applicable'. Targets can remain 201-1-a/a-i/a-ii/a-iii/b because the EVG&D source basis applies to the reported EVG&D data.

missing_item_templates: plans and timeline when financial implication or cost calculation system is unavailable
applicability of no-system fallback
guardrail_effects: when no calculation system exists, require plans and timeline instead of a generic method guardrail
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 201-2-a, GRI 201-2-a-iii, and GRI 201-2-a-v. This rule is a conditional fallback when no calculation system exists; use requires_unavailable_data_plan plus an applicability condition, not a general requires_method_or_assumption guardrail.

missing_item_templates: monetary value of government financial assistance
GAAP or consistent accounting basis
guardrail_effects: do not mark financial assistance disclosed when monetary value or accounting basis is missing
rules.review_note: Remove GRI 201-4-c from targets; the GAAP monetary-value rule applies to 201-4-a, a-i through a-viii, and 201-4-b. Remove requires_gender_breakdown and replace it with a monetary-amount/accounting-basis facet.

missing_item_templates: percentage calculated using full-time employee data
guardrail_effects: local senior management percentage must use full-time employee denominator
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 202-2-a. The full-time-employee denominator does not govern the definition leaves 202-2-b/c/d. Keep requires_percentage and a full-time-employee denominator/scope facet; remove requires_employee_category_breakdown.

missing_item_templates: audited consolidated financial statement/public record source basis
period covered by the most recent audited statements or permitted fallback period
guardrail_effects: country-by-country data period must align with audited consolidated statements or permitted fallback period
rules.review_note: Remove requires_gender_breakdown and requires_unavailable_data_plan. Replace with requires_source_basis plus reporting-period alignment/fallback-period context. The targets may remain disclosure-wide because the prescribed financial-statement period applies to all 207-4 data.

missing_item_templates: reconciliation to audited consolidated statements for selected fields
explanation for reconciliation differences
guardrail_effects: reconciliation guardrail applies only to selected financial fields, not unrelated country-by-country leaves
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 207-4-b-iv, b-vi, b-vii, and b-viii. Add requires_reconciliation and requires_difference_explanation; remove requires_gender_breakdown. Do not apply this reconciliation rule to unrelated country-by-country leaves.

missing_item_templates: exclude deferred corporate income tax and uncertain tax position provisions
current tax accrued for reported period
guardrail_effects: current tax accrued must exclude deferred tax and uncertain-tax-position provisions
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 207-4-b-ix. Remove requires_percentage. Keep an exclusion-scope/reporting-period facet: current tax accrued must exclude deferred tax and uncertain-tax-position provisions.

missing_item_templates: stateless entity reported separately
tax jurisdiction boundary
guardrail_effects: stateless entities require separate reporting and should not be folded into ordinary jurisdiction rows
rules.review_note: Exclude GRI 207-4-c from targets; apply separate reporting to GRI 207-4-a and the 207-4-b data for a stateless entity. Replace requires_percentage with requires_separate_reporting / requires_tax_jurisdiction_boundary.

missing_item_templates: total material weight or volume used as denominator consistent with 301-1
guardrail_effects: block recycled-input percentage when denominator basis is absent
rules.review_note: Target is correct, but replace requires_count_or_amount with requires_denominator_basis. Missing item should be 'total material weight or volume used as the denominator, consistent with 301-1'; guardrail should block a recycled-input percentage when that denominator basis is absent.

missing_item_templates: recycled input material percentage formula
guardrail_effects: recycled input disclosure requires formula-based percentage

missing_item_templates: exclude rejects and recalls of products
guardrail_effects: reclaimed product percentage should exclude rejects and recalls
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 301-3-a. The rejects/recalls exclusion affects the reclaimed-product percentage, not the separate data-collection-method leaf GRI 301-3-b.

missing_item_templates: percentage of reclaimed products and packaging by product category
guardrail_effects: formula governs reclaimed product and packaging percentage leaf only
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 301-3-a. The formula governs the percentage leaf; it does not itself satisfy or constrain GRI 301-3-b on how data were collected.

missing_item_templates: avoid double-counting self-generated energy
guardrail_effects: self-generated energy must be counted once under fuel consumption
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-1-a, b, c, and e. Replace requires_percentage with a no-double-counting/calculation-boundary facet. Do not apply this rule to energy sold, method disclosure, or conversion-factor source leaves.

missing_item_templates: separate non-renewable and renewable fuel consumption
guardrail_effects: fuel consumption must be split by non-renewable and renewable sources
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 302-1-a and GRI 302-1-b. Replace requires_percentage with requires_energy_source_breakdown / requires_separate_reporting for renewable versus non-renewable fuel.

missing_item_templates: entities owned or controlled by the organization
guardrail_effects: internal energy consumption must be limited to owned or controlled entities
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-1-a, b, c, and e. Replace requires_worker_boundary with requires_organizational_boundary (entities owned or controlled). Do not apply to energy sold or method/factor-source leaves.

missing_item_templates: total internal energy formula in joules or multiples
guardrail_effects: total internal energy needs formula and unit basis
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-1-e and, if methodology sufficiency is being modeled, GRI 302-1-f. The total-energy formula does not govern the individual fuel, purchased-energy, sold-energy, or conversion-factor-source leaves.

missing_item_templates: exclude energy already reported in 302-1
guardrail_effects: external energy consumption must exclude internal energy reported under 302-1
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-2-a and GRI 302-2-b. The exclusion of energy already reported in 302-1 affects the outside-organization amount and its compilation method, not the conversion-factor source leaf.

missing_item_templates: absolute energy numerator and organization-specific denominator
guardrail_effects: energy intensity ratio requires numerator and denominator
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-3-a and GRI 302-3-b. The numerator/denominator formula does not directly govern the energy-type leaf or the within/outside boundary leaf.

missing_item_templates: separate internal and external energy intensity ratios
guardrail_effects: internal and external energy intensity ratios require separate reporting when both are reported
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-3-a and GRI 302-3-d. Add a separate-reporting/boundary facet; the rule does not directly govern the denominator metric or energy-type list.

missing_item_templates: exclude reductions from reduced production capacity or outsourcing
guardrail_effects: energy reduction cannot rely on reduced production capacity or outsourcing effects
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-4-a and GRI 302-4-d. The exclusion affects the reduction amount and its methodology, not the energy-type or baseline leaf.

missing_item_templates: whether reduction is estimated, modeled, or directly measured
method used for estimation or modeling
guardrail_effects: energy reduction evidence needs measurement basis and method when estimated or modeled
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 302-4-a and GRI 302-4-d. Remove requires_gender_breakdown. Keep requires_method_or_assumption and specify measurement basis: estimated, modeled, or directly measured.

missing_item_templates: public and credible water stress assessment tool or methodology
guardrail_effects: water-stress withdrawal branch requires credible water-stress methodology
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to the water-stress withdrawal branch GRI 303-3-b and b-i through b-v, plus GRI 303-3-d for methodology context. Do not apply the water-stress assessment method to all-area totals or salinity breakdown leaves.

missing_item_templates: public and credible water stress assessment tool or methodology
guardrail_effects: water discharge to water-stressed areas requires credible methodology
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 303-4-c, c-i, c-ii, and GRI 303-4-e. The water-stress assessment method does not govern all discharge destinations, substance-of-concern leaves, or non-water-stress totals.

missing_item_templates: exclude GHG trades from gross Scope 1 emissions
guardrail_effects: Scope 1 gross emissions must exclude GHG trades
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-1-a and GRI 305-1-g. The GHG-trade exclusion affects the gross Scope 1 amount and its method, not base-year, factor-source, gas-list, or consolidation leaves.

missing_item_templates: biogenic CO2 reported separately
specified biogenic non-CO2 and lifecycle emissions excluded
guardrail_effects: gross Scope 1 evidence must not mix biogenic emissions into direct emissions
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-1-a and the checklist leaf containing biogenic CO2 (currently GRI 305-1-d). Replace requires_percentage with requires_separate_reporting; retain requires_exclusion_scope for non-CO2 biogenic gases and non-combustion/biodegradation lifecycle emissions.

missing_item_templates: exclude GHG trades from gross Scope 2 emissions
guardrail_effects: Scope 2 gross emissions must exclude GHG trades
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-2-a, GRI 305-2-b, and GRI 305-2-g. The GHG-trade exclusion does not govern gas-list, base-year, factor-source, or consolidation leaves.

missing_item_templates: exclude Scope 3 emissions from Scope 2 disclosure
guardrail_effects: Scope 2 evidence must not include Scope 3 emissions
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-2-a, GRI 305-2-b, and GRI 305-2-g. The Scope 3 exclusion is part of the Scope 2 boundary/method, not a guardrail for every 305-2 leaf.

missing_item_templates: location-based method when product or supplier-specific data is unavailable
guardrail_effects: Scope 2 method depends on availability of contractual/product-specific data
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-2-a, GRI 305-2-b, and GRI 305-2-g. Replace requires_percentage and requires_region_breakdown with requires_method_or_assumption plus an applicability condition for markets without contractual/product-specific data.

missing_item_templates: location-based and market-based methods when contractual instruments exist
guardrail_effects: Scope 2 market-based method applies when contractual instruments are available
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-2-a, GRI 305-2-b, and GRI 305-2-g. Remove requires_percentage, requires_gender_breakdown, and requires_region_breakdown; use method plus applicability for markets with contractual instruments.

missing_item_templates: exclude GHG trades from gross Scope 3 emissions
guardrail_effects: Scope 3 gross emissions must exclude GHG trades
rules.review_note: Narrow target to GRI 305-3-a (and a methodology leaf only if the checklist later restores one). The GHG-trade exclusion does not govern gases, base year, factor source, or other unrelated leaves.

missing_item_templates: exclude Scope 2 emissions from Scope 3 disclosure
guardrail_effects: Scope 3 evidence must not include Scope 2 emissions
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-3-a and the categories/activities leaf GRI 305-3-d. Do not apply the Scope 2 exclusion to base-year or factor-source leaves.

missing_item_templates: absolute GHG emissions numerator and organization-specific denominator
guardrail_effects: GHG intensity ratio requires numerator and denominator
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-4-a and GRI 305-4-b. The ratio formula does not directly govern the list of Scope types included.

missing_item_templates: Scope 3 intensity separately from Scope 1 and Scope 2 intensity
guardrail_effects: Scope 3 intensity must be reported separately from Scope 1/2 when included
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-4-a and GRI 305-4-c. The separate Scope 3 ratio rule does not directly govern the denominator metric.

missing_item_templates: exclude reductions from reduced production capacity or outsourcing
guardrail_effects: GHG reduction cannot rely on reduced production capacity or outsourcing effects
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-5-a and GRI 305-5-e. The production-capacity/outsourcing exclusion affects the reduction amount and its methodology, not gas types or Scope labels.

missing_item_templates: inventory or project accounting method
guardrail_effects: GHG reduction amount requires inventory or project method basis
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-5-a and GRI 305-5-e. Inventory/project accounting method does not govern gas types or Scope labels.

missing_item_templates: primary effects plus significant secondary effects
guardrail_effects: GHG reduction total requires primary and significant secondary effects method
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-5-a and GRI 305-5-e. Primary and significant secondary effects affect the calculated reduction and methodology only.

missing_item_templates: reductions reported separately by Scope type
guardrail_effects: when multiple Scopes are reported, reductions must be split by Scope type
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-5-a and GRI 305-5-d. Replace requires_percentage with requires_scope_breakdown / requires_separate_reporting; the rule requires separate reductions by Scope, not a percentage.

missing_item_templates: offset-derived reductions reported separately
guardrail_effects: offset reductions must be separate from reduction initiatives
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 305-5-a and GRI 305-5-e. Replace requires_percentage with requires_separate_reporting for offset-derived reductions.

missing_item_templates: ODS production minus approved destruction and feedstock use
guardrail_effects: ODS production amount requires specified calculation basis
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 305-6-a. The production formula does not govern the substances-included or emission-factor-source leaves.

missing_item_templates: direct measurement of significant air emissions
emission-factor source may be not applicable when direct measurement is used
guardrail_effects: direct measurement can satisfy method; factor-source leaf may be not applicable
rules.review_note: Remove requires_gender_breakdown. Use requires_method_or_assumption with a direct-measurement evidence kind. If GRI 305-7-b remains targeted, the guardrail must state that emission-factor source may be not applicable when direct measurement is used.

missing_item_templates: calculation based on site-specific data
guardrail_effects: significant air emissions can use site-specific data calculation

missing_item_templates: calculation based on published emission factors
guardrail_effects: significant air emissions can use published emission factors

missing_item_templates: basis of estimation when default figures are unavailable
guardrail_effects: estimated significant air emissions require estimation basis
rules.review_note: Replace requires_compilation_context with requires_method_or_assumption plus requires_estimation_basis. Missing item should be the basis of estimation when default figures are unavailable; the generic context guardrail is not actionable.

missing_item_templates: exclude effluent unless national law requires inclusion in total waste
guardrail_effects: do not count effluent in total waste unless required by law
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 306-3-a. Replace the guardrail with 'do not count effluent in total waste unless national law requires it'; the source does not require a separate narrative explanation for GRI 306-3-b.

missing_item_templates: 1000 kilograms per metric ton or metric tons directly
guardrail_effects: waste weight conversion uses 1000 kg per metric ton when needed
rules.review_note: Target only GRI 306-3-a. Replace requires_compilation_context with requires_unit_conversion; accept metric tons directly or convert using 1,000 kg per metric ton.

missing_item_templates: exclude effluent unless national law requires inclusion in total waste
guardrail_effects: do not count effluent as diverted waste unless required by law
rules.review_note: Exclude GRI 306-4-e from targets; apply to all quantitative weight leaves GRI 306-4-a through d-ii. Guardrail should prevent totals that include effluent unless required by law, rather than require a standalone exclusion explanation.

missing_item_templates: 1000 kilograms per metric ton or metric tons directly
guardrail_effects: diverted waste weight conversion uses 1000 kg per metric ton when needed
rules.review_note: Exclude GRI 306-4-e from targets; apply to all quantitative weight leaves. Replace requires_compilation_context with requires_unit_conversion using 1,000 kg per metric ton.

missing_item_templates: exclude effluent unless national law requires inclusion in total waste
guardrail_effects: do not count effluent as disposed waste unless required by law
rules.review_note: Exclude GRI 306-5-e from targets; apply to all quantitative weight leaves GRI 306-5-a through d-ii. Guardrail should prevent totals that include effluent unless required by law, not demand a separate explanation.

missing_item_templates: total employee numbers at reporting period end for hire and turnover rates
guardrail_effects: hire and turnover rates require period-end employee denominator

missing_item_templates: exclude in-kind general welfare programs
guardrail_effects: general welfare facilities, meals, and similar programs should not be counted as listed benefits
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 401-2-a and GRI 401-2-a-vii. The in-kind-benefit exclusion does not govern the named benefit leaves or the definition of significant locations.

missing_item_templates: exclude fatalities from high-consequence injury count and rate
guardrail_effects: high-consequence injury metrics exclude fatalities
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 403-9-a-ii, GRI 403-9-b-ii, and GRI 403-9-g. This exclusion applies only to high-consequence injury number/rate calculations, not all injury, hazard, hour, or worker-exclusion leaves.

missing_item_templates: include fatalities in recordable injury count and rate
guardrail_effects: recordable injury metrics include fatalities from work-related injury
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 403-9-a-iii, GRI 403-9-b-iii, and GRI 403-9-g. Fatalities are included in recordable-injury calculations only; do not apply this rule to high-consequence, hours, hazards, or exclusion leaves.

missing_item_templates: commuting incidents included only when transport is organization-arranged
guardrail_effects: commuting incidents count only when organization-arranged transport is involved
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 403-9-a-i through a-iv, GRI 403-9-b-i through b-iv, and GRI 403-9-g. Replace generic compilation context with an injury-scope/commuting-incident boundary facet.

missing_item_templates: rates based on 200000 or 1000000 hours worked
guardrail_effects: injury rates require explicit hours-worked denominator basis
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to the rate-bearing leaves GRI 403-9-a-i/a-ii/a-iii, GRI 403-9-b-i/b-ii/b-iii, plus GRI 403-9-e and GRI 403-9-g. Do not apply the rate basis to hours, injury types, hazards, controls, or worker-exclusion leaves.

missing_item_templates: include fatalities in recordable work-related ill health cases
guardrail_effects: recordable ill-health case calculations include work-related ill-health fatalities
rules.review_note: Narrow targets to GRI 403-10-a-ii, GRI 403-10-b-ii, and GRI 403-10-e. The rule governs recordable ill-health case calculations, not fatality counts, illness types, hazards, controls, or worker exclusions.

missing_item_templates: specified discrimination grounds, internal/external stakeholders, operations, and reporting period
guardrail_effects: discrimination incident scope must align with protected grounds and stakeholder boundary
rules.review_note: Replace requires_percentage with an incident-scope facet covering the specified discrimination grounds, internal/external stakeholders, operations, and reporting period. Missing item and guardrail must test scope alignment, not a percentage or rate.

missing_item_templates: exclude incidents where organization was determined not at fault
guardrail_effects: zero product-safety non-compliance statement is valid only after no-fault exclusion
rules.review_note: Keep the targets, but replace the generic 'exclusions must be explained' guardrail with: incidents where the organization was determined not at fault must not be counted; a zero-event statement is valid only after this exclusion.

missing_item_templates: exclude labeling-related non-compliance from 416-2 and route to 417-2
guardrail_effects: product safety non-compliance must not include labeling incidents
rules.review_note: Keep the targets, but make the guardrail specific: labeling-related non-compliance must be excluded from 416-2 and routed to 417-2. Do not require a generic exclusion narrative.

missing_item_templates: identify current-period non-compliance related to prior-period events
guardrail_effects: zero product-safety non-compliance statement cannot omit prior-period event attribution
rules.review_note: Keep the disclosure-wide targets, but replace the generic applicability guardrail with an actionable temporal rule: when current-period non-compliance relates to prior-period events, identify it; a zero-event statement cannot ignore such incidents.

missing_item_templates: exclude incidents where organization was determined not at fault
guardrail_effects: zero labeling non-compliance statement is valid only after no-fault exclusion
rules.review_note: Keep the targets, but replace the generic guardrail with: incidents where the organization was determined not at fault must not be counted; a zero-event statement is valid only after this exclusion.

missing_item_templates: identify current-period labeling non-compliance related to prior-period events
guardrail_effects: zero labeling non-compliance statement cannot omit prior-period event attribution
rules.review_note: Keep the disclosure-wide targets, but make the guardrail specific: identify current-period non-compliance incidents arising from prior-period events; a zero-event statement cannot omit them.

missing_item_templates: exclude incidents where organization was determined not at fault
guardrail_effects: zero marketing non-compliance statement is valid only after no-fault exclusion
rules.review_note: Keep the targets, but replace the generic guardrail with: incidents where the organization was determined not at fault must not be counted; a zero-event statement is valid only after this exclusion.

missing_item_templates: identify current-period marketing non-compliance related to prior-period events
guardrail_effects: zero marketing non-compliance statement cannot omit prior-period event attribution
rules.review_note: Keep the disclosure-wide targets, but make the guardrail specific: identify current-period marketing-communication non-compliance arising from prior-period events; a zero-event statement cannot omit them.

missing_item_templates: substantial number of privacy breaches related to preceding years
guardrail_effects: privacy breach count must state substantial prior-year attribution when applicable; do not apply to zero-complaint statement leaf 418-1-c
rules.review_note: Remove GRI 418-1-c from targets; apply to GRI 418-1-a, a-i, a-ii, and b. Replace requires_count_or_amount with requires_prior_period_attribution / temporal context. Guardrail: if a substantial number of breaches relate to preceding years, that fact must be stated.
